title: Affordable and strong.
text: Bought it a few years ago.  Replaced the battery about 6 months ago, It is not fragile, I do everything I need to on it.  I would buy it again. If you looking for a lap top that shows movies well, hooks up to HDMI, does several different functions at a time, it is great.  For a kid for school or any thing else...REALLY great for the cost. At this rate EVERY kid could have one.A friend had one that he kept alive for ever.  It would  not give out.  He bought an ASUS right before I bought mine, and his stopped working about a year ago.

text: Worked great out of the box. Just plugged things in.  The fan is louder than the cooler master I sent back (didn't work), but I'll try the Razor for awhile and replace the fan if it starts to bother me.  The LG laptop is a fantastic deal at Costco.  For good battery life, you can't have the Nvidia card in the laptop--because they suck the power.  I have the Intel IrisXE graphics when I'm on the road, but if I'm doing some heavy CAD work at home the Razor is perfect. 4 USB ports on the back make this a great docking station. I now just have one USB-C connecting my laptop via Thunderbolt.  Couldn't be happier.

title: Get the Core X NOT THE CHROMA (performance loss tho)
text: I don't recommend the Chroma, if you are willing to take the performance loss get a Core X. I paired this with a Dell XPS 9570 (16GB/1TB NVME)/ROG-STRIX-RTX-2080TI-O11G. I had purchased the Chroma version for Ethernet and USB not the lights, unfortunately the Ethernet port drops all the time even with the drivers from the chip mfg website. If you plug in stuff to USB the Ethernet will also drop. I returned the Chroma version for the regular Core X, then I returned the Core X too. I couldn't justify how much performance loss there was, ended up building a new system with a 3900X.
ptitle: Razer Core X Aluminum External GPU Enclosure (eGPU): Compatible with Windows & MacOS Thunderbolt 3 Laptops, NVIDIA /AMD PCIe Support, 650W PSU, Mercury White

title: Easy Peasy Plug and play on Mac that works!

Awesome, significant boost of graphics, easy as plugging in to Thunderbolt port and turning on.  No drivers to update, Big Sur recognized card and put it to use. title: Probably works well, as long as you really need it and have the right cable.
text: It does not work without the Thunderbolt plugged in, and the Apple Thunderbolt cable is not good enough.  Other than that it works fine.  I planned to use it on my Macbook for both Mac OS and Windows when dual booted.  As others probably know, this doesn't actually work.  You can work around it if you're very motivated, but it's a fair amount of technical work (think scripting and system configuaration settings) and will always have challenges.  Plus, then you're not supported.  Fine if that's what you want, but then it's a pretty expensive dock.
